objective: Measure why Wave 47 exact metadata support stops at 13 value-unique rows instead of the 60-row diagnostic target.
non_negotiables: This is a diagnostic report, not a new promoted shard.
The report must preserve selected topology counts and rejection counts.
Exact support scale must not be inferred from heuristic support.
The next generator wave must target the measured component-value reuse bottleneck.

tasks.prompt: Add a value-unique signature profile search report command that runs the same selector used by the Wave 47 exact shard and exposes selected counts and rejection reasons.
outputs: Regression test for the 13-row support limit
acceptance: The report uses the existing signature support report schema.
The rpf20 value-unique selector reports 13 selected rows.
Rejection counts include component_value_digest_reuse_before_materialization as the dominant blocker.

tasks.prompt: Run the value-unique signature support report for rows-per-family 20 and record the artifact hash.
acceptance: The report has 90 left and 92 right signature profiles.
The report scans 8280 candidate pairs per topology family.
Selected counts are 4 asymmetric-fan, 5 local-move, and 4 pawn-phalanx.
Rejection counts record 18391 component-value reuse rejections.

tasks.prompt: Record Wave 48 as the exact-support bottleneck measurement and set the next wave goal to generate new component-value diversity.
acceptance: Docs distinguish Wave 48 diagnostics from Wave 47 exact rows.
Claims identify component-value reuse pressure as the immediate exact support blocker.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_48_value_unique_signature_support_diagnostic.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 48 plan is internally consistent.
The support-collapse report records the current value-unique bottleneck.
